core_payload.content:
# Symphony Observability

## 1. Dream Direction
The Dream branch imagines Symphony Observability as a richer evidence layer for orchestration.

## 2. Desired Future State
- stronger assignment-aware status views instead of anonymous run lists.
- clearer cost, rate-limit, and lane attribution for each session family.
- activity filters that work across terminal, web, mobile, control-surface, and control-plane views.
- easier projection of review requests, failures, degraded modes, and follow-up ownership into operator surfaces.

## 3. Why It Matters
Dream preserves the idea that a trustworthy issue runner must be able to explain not only what it ran, but on whose behalf it ran, what it cost, which lane did the work, and what requires intervention next.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Observability in the Dream branch preserves a richer evidence layer for orchestration. It imagines assignment-aware status views, clearer cost and lane attribution, cross-client activity filters, and stronger projection of review requests, failures, degraded modes, and fallback recoveries into operator surfaces. The capsule matters because a trustworthy issue runner must explain not only what it ran, but on whose behalf it ran, what it cost, which lane carried the work, whether the result needed recovery, and what needs intervention next.
